content: Remove obsolete .gitignore entries when tools are deprecated, but preserve entries explicitly required by project coordinators

Archive obsolete feature branches by extracting their unique content to archive/ subdirectories. For streamlit-frontend branches, archived complete frontend code and documentation to archive/streamlit-frontend/ preserving full implementation history.

Code cleanup requires comprehensive plan document listing all files to delete, merge, or modify. Include phased execution with risk mitigation, verification commands, and success criteria. Created docs/plans/CLEANUP_PLAN.md addressing 8 technical debt areas identified in backend analysis.

name: dx-modernization-workflow
description: DX modernization: Create pyproject.toml from requirements.txt for uv migration. Pin deps to exact versions. Fix start.sh paths when frontend is at src/frontend/. Add typecheck script to package.json. Note: builder scope overlap is common - document overlapping files in merge_ready.

content: Code cleanup workflow: 4-phase execution (safe removals → layer consolidation → security hardening → code quality fixes) minimizes risk by ordering changes from low to high impact

Python dependency management: Use pyproject.toml for dependency specification (with ranges) and requirements.txt for pinned versions. This dual approach supports both flexible development (pip install -e .) and reproducible deployments (pip install -r requirements.txt). Pin all versions in requirements.txt using == for exact reproducibility.

Document broken tests before cleanup: When discovering tests with import errors from old architecture (e.g., deleted 'frontend' or 'backend' modules), create BROKEN_TESTS.md documenting each broken test file with status (DELETE or NEEDS_REVIEW) before removing them. Run 'pytest --collect-only' to identify import errors. Total test count and working test count helps assess cleanup impact. Prevents accidental deletion of salvageable tests.
Root directory cleanup: Archive test data (embeddings/, examples/) rather than deleting to preserve historical context. Move utility scripts to scripts/ directory for better organization.

pyproject.toml supersedes pytest.ini: When pyproject.toml has [tool.pytest.ini_options] section, pytest.ini is redundant and should be deleted. Verify pytest config in pyproject.toml is complete before deleting pytest.ini.
